@@ -110,8 +110,8 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8z5","title":"Create report_skipped_tools() post-install summary","description":"# Task: Create report_skipped_tools() post-install summary ## Context Part of EPIC: Checkpoint-Based Checksum Recovery (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-tx7) ## What to Do After installation completes, report any tools that were skipped: ### Output ``` ⚠ The following tools were skipped due to checksum mismatches: → ntm: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../install.sh → bv: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../install.sh You can install these manually after verifying they are safe. Or wait for ACFS to update checksums and run: acfs update --stack ``` ### Storage - SKIPPED_TOOLS array populated during install - Also saved to state.json for persistence ## Acceptance Criteria - Only shown if SKIPPED_TOOLS is non-empty - Shows tool name and installer URL - Provides manual install command - Mentions acfs update as alternative ## Files to Modify - install.sh: Add report at end of successful install","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:44:38.550459-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:48:42.459331-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:48:42.459331-05:00","close_reason":"Added comprehensive skipped tools reporting: record_skipped_tool() with reason and URL, report_skipped_tools() showing detailed summary with manual install commands and acfs update alternative, get_skipped_tools_json() for state persistence.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8z5","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4jr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:33.367598-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-96cx","title":"TASK: Add 'Your First 5 Minutes' section to launch-onboarding","description":"## Parent Feature agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-trkz (Post-Installation Onboarding Experience) ## Summary Add a prominent, step-by-step "Your First 5 Minutes" walkthrough to the launch-onboarding page that guides users through their first Claude Code interaction. ## Rationale The UX audit identified the critical "now what?" gap: > After "Congratulations!", users see \`cc\` command but don't know: > - That they need to authenticate first > - What folder to be in > - What to actually ask Claude This task creates a hand-holding first experience. ## Content Specification ### Section: "Your First 5 Minutes" Place this BEFORE the "What you can do now" cards. \`\`\`tsx

Your First 5 Minutes

Let's make sure everything works with a quick test run.

{/* Step 1 */}
1

Create a project folder

{/* Step 2 */}
2

Authenticate Claude

A browser window will open. Log in with your Anthropic account, then return here.

{/* Step 3 */}

Start Claude Code

After authenticating, this shortcut launches Claude Code.

{/* Step 4 */}

Your first prompt

In the Claude prompt, type:

Create a simple Python script that prints "Hello from AI!" and run it
{/* Step 5 */}

Watch the magic!

Claude will:

  • ✓ Create a file called hello.py
  • ✓ Write the Python code
  • ✓ Run the script for you
  • ✓ Show "Hello from AI!" in the output

🎉 Congratulations! You just used AI to write and run code!

\`\`\` ### Visual Design - Large, numbered steps (1-5) - Each step has clear heading - Commands have copy buttons - Expected outcomes are listed - Celebration at the end ### Mobile Considerations - Stack vertically on mobile - Numbers should be smaller but still visible - Copy buttons must work on touch ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] "Your First 5 Minutes" section exists - [ ] 5 clear, numbered steps - [ ] Each step has command and explanation - [ ] Authentication step sets expectations (browser opens) - [ ] Expected Claude behavior listed - [ ] Celebration message at end - [ ] All commands have copy buttons - [ ] Mobile-friendly layout ## Dependencies - FEATURE: Wizard Password-First Flow Updates (ensures users reach this page) ## Files Modified - apps/web/app/wizard/launch-onboarding/page.tsx","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:21:23.424815-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:43:18.529572-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:43:18.529572-05:00","close_reason":"Your First 5 Minutes section implemented by FuchsiaHill. 5 numbered steps with authentication guidance, example prompt, expected outcomes, and celebration message. All quality gates pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-96cx","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-trkz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:21:55.313779-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-96cx","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9ntj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:21:55.499814-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-998","title":"Fix web build: commit missing Jargon component/dictionary","description":"apps/web/app/page.tsx imports @/components/jargon and @/lib/jargon but these files are missing from git (currently present only as untracked files). This breaks build/type-check/lint in a clean clone. Track the files, fix any lint/type issues, and ensure reduced-motion and keyboard accessibility are respected.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GreenDog","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:36:58.073784-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:44:58.191838-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T20:44:58.191838-05:00","close_reason":"Resolved on main: landing page + jargon component/dictionary now tracked and wired (see commit 1ef012c). {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9ntj","title":"TASK: Create /wizard/verify-key-connection page","description":"## Parent Feature agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t66c (Wizard Password-First Flow Updates) ## Summary Create a new wizard page that guides users to verify their SSH key-based connection works after the installer has set it up. This closes the loop on the password-first flow and proves the key installation succeeded. ## Rationale After the installer prompts for and installs the SSH key, users need to: 1. Disconnect from the password-based session 2. Reconnect using their SSH key 3. Verify it works without password This step is crucial because: - Confirms key was installed correctly - Teaches user the "correct" connection method - Provides troubleshooting if key doesn't work ## Page Content ### Header "Verify Key-Based Connection" ~1 min ### Main Content \`\`\`tsx // Step 1: Disconnect

Step 1: Disconnect

Type 'exit' and press Enter to close your current session:

Your terminal should return to your local computer prompt.

// Step 2: Reconnect with key

Step 2: Reconnect with your SSH key

Now connect using your SSH key instead of password:

// Success indicator
  • No password prompt appeared
  • You see: ubuntu@vps:~$
If you had to enter a password, something went wrong - see troubleshooting below. \`\`\` ### Troubleshooting Section Common issues and fixes: 1. **Still asks for password** - Key may not have been pasted correctly during installer - Re-run installer: it will prompt for key again 2. **Permission denied (publickey)** - Key file permissions might be wrong - Run: \`chmod 600 ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519\` 3. **Connection refused** - VPS may have rebooted - Wait 1-2 minutes and try again ### SimplerGuide Content Explain: - Why we verify this step - What "key-based authentication" means - Why no password is good (more secure, more convenient) ## Technical Details ### File Location \`apps/web/app/wizard/verify-key-connection/page.tsx\` ### Step Number This becomes step 8 in the new flow (after run-installer). ### URL \`/wizard/verify-key-connection\` ### Navigation - Previous: run-installer (or status-check) - Next: accounts ### State Requirements - Needs vpsIP from localStorage (same as ssh-connect page) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] New page exists at /wizard/verify-key-connection - [ ] Shows exit command first - [ ] Shows key-based SSH command - [ ] Success criteria clearly stated - [ ] Troubleshooting for common key issues - [ ] SimplerGuide explains the "why" - [ ] Proper navigation (prev/next) - [ ] Step number updated in wizardSteps.ts ## Dependencies - TASK: Update /wizard/ssh-connect (page flow order) - TASK: Integrate SSH prompt into installer (key must be installed first) ## Files Modified - apps/web/app/wizard/verify-key-connection/page.tsx (new file) - apps/web/lib/wizardSteps.ts (add new step) - Related pages (update navigation)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:20:51.279962-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:40:27.19223-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:40:27.19223-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9ntj","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hy7z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:21:22.933443-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9ntj","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-egf5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:21:23.092203-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9x2","title":"Wizard: Windows SSH key commands have broken backslashes","description":"apps/web/app/wizard/generate-ssh-key/page.tsx uses windowsCommand strings with single backslashes (e.g., /Users/jemanuel\.ssh\...), which are treated as JS escapes and drop the backslashes. Fix to use double-escaped backslashes so the copied command is valid on Windows.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T14:03:29.105181-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T14:04:03.163713-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T14:04:03.163713-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed Windows command strings in generate-ssh-key step to properly escape backslashes (so copied commands use /Users/jemanuel\.ssh\... instead of dropping slashes)."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9xt","title":"[ubu.1] Create ubuntu_upgrade.sh library","description":"# Create ubuntu_upgrade.sh Library ## Purpose Core library implementing Ubuntu upgrade detection and execution logic. ## File Location `scripts/lib/ubuntu_upgrade.sh` ## Functions to Implement ### Version Detection ```bash # Get current Ubuntu version as comparable number (e.g., 2404 for 24.04) ubuntu_get_version_number() # Get current Ubuntu version string (e.g., "24.04") ubuntu_get_version_string() # Compare two version numbers (returns 0 if $1 >= $2) ubuntu_version_gte() { local v1="$1" v2="$2"; } ``` ### Upgrade Path Calculation ```bash # Get next available upgrade version (queries do-release-upgrade -c) ubuntu_get_next_upgrade() # Calculate full upgrade path from current to target # Returns newline-separated list of versions ubuntu_calculate_upgrade_path() { local target="$1"; } # Get the latest available Ubuntu version ubuntu_get_latest_available() ``` ### Pre-upgrade Checks ```bash # Run all pre-upgrade validations # Returns 0 if all checks pass, 1 with error details if not ubuntu_preflight_checks() # Individual checks (called by ubuntu_preflight_checks) ubuntu_check_disk_space() # Need ~5GB free ubuntu_check_network() # Test connectivity ubuntu_check_apt_state() # No broken packages ubuntu_check_not_docker() # Upgrades don't work in containers ``` ### Upgrade Execution ```bash # Perform single-version upgrade # Returns 0 on success, 1 on failure ubuntu_do_upgrade() # Prepare system before upgrade (apt update, dist-upgrade) ubuntu_prepare_upgrade() # Setup resume mechanism for after reboot ubuntu_setup_resume() # Cleanup resume mechanism after completion ubuntu_cleanup_resume() ``` ## Dependencies - Sources: logging.sh, os_detect.sh - External: do-release-upgrade, apt, lsb_release ## Design Decisions ### Why query do-release-upgrade instead of hardcoding? Hardcoding version numbers (like "25.10") becomes stale. By querying `do-release-upgrade -c` (check mode), we always know the actual next available upgrade. This future-proofs the code. ### Why check disk space? do-release-upgrade downloads hundreds of MB of packages and needs space for temporary files. Running out of disk mid-upgrade is catastrophic. We require 5GB free (conservative estimate). ### Why not use unattended-upgrade? unattended-upgrade is for security patches within a version, not for full distribution upgrades. do-release-upgrade is the official tool for version upgrades. ## Testing Notes - Test on 22.04, 24.04, 24.10 base images - Verify upgrade path calculation is correct - Ensure preflight checks catch common issues","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:19:52.130051-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:32:12.108731-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:32:12.108731-05:00","close_reason":"Created ubuntu_upgrade.sh with all core functions: version detection, upgrade path calculation, preflight checks, and upgrade execution. All 20 functions implemented and tested."} @@ -149,7 +149,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-db0","title":"Implement shell tool updates (omz, p10k, plugins, atuin, zoxide)","description":"## What Implement the 'shell' category for acfs-update: - Oh-My-Zsh: Run $ZSH/tools/upgrade.sh (non-interactive) - Powerlevel10k: git pull --ff-only in $ZSH_CUSTOM/themes/powerlevel10k - zsh-autosuggestions: git pull --ff-only in plugin dir - zsh-syntax-highlighting: git pull --ff-only in plugin dir - Atuin: Use atuin self-update if available, else reinstall - Zoxide: Reinstall via official installer ## Git-Based Updates (omz, p10k, plugins) - Use git pull --ff-only to avoid merge conflicts - If fast-forward fails: warn and skip (user has local changes) - OMZ has its own upgrade script that handles prompts ## Installer-Based Updates (Atuin, Zoxide) These use curl|sh installers like the stack tools: 1. Atuin: Try `atuin self-update` first (if available) - If no self-update: fetch installer, verify checksum, run 2. Zoxide: Fetch installer, verify checksum against checksums.yaml, run For checksum mismatches: same flow as stack tools (warn, prompt, skip/proceed) ## Considerations - ZSH_CUSTOM defaults to $ZSH/custom if not set - DISABLE_UPDATE_PROMPT=true for OMZ non-interactive - Some users may have customized themes/plugins - respect that ## Success Criteria - [ ] All shell tools update without prompts (in --yes mode) - [ ] Git-based updates handle conflicts gracefully - [ ] Atuin/Zoxide checksum verification works - [ ] Before/after versions logged - [ ] Skips missing components","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:10.463809-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:18:10.575757-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:18:10.575757-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented shell tool updates: omz, p10k, zsh plugins, atuin, zoxide","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-db0","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:25.018434-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dcy","title":"[codex.8] Audit web app for incorrect codex auth guidance","description":"## Goal Check apps/web/ for any incorrect guidance about codex-cli authentication. ## Files to Check Based on grep results: - apps/web/lib/jargon.ts - apps/web/app/wizard/run-installer/page.tsx - apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts - apps/web/app/workflow/page.tsx - apps/web/app/page.tsx ## What to Look For 1. Any mention of OPENAI_API_KEY for codex 2. Incorrect auth flow descriptions 3. Wizard steps that suggest API key setup ## Updates Needed 1. Replace API key guidance with OAuth flow description 2. Update any wizard steps about codex setup 3. Ensure jargon/definitions are correct ## Lower Priority Because The web app is less critical than the installer scripts - users who hit the web app will eventually run the installer which (after fixes) will give correct guidance. ## Acceptance Criteria 1. No incorrect OPENAI_API_KEY references for codex in web app 2. Correct OAuth flow described if mentioned","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:24.840054-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:45:56.25612-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:45:56.25612-05:00","close_reason":"Audited web app - no incorrect OPENAI_API_KEY references for codex found. Jargon entry describes Codex without auth details. CAAM references are about credential management (correct).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dcy","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ua5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:36.94908-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dfc","title":"Fix wizard layout heading causing mobile E2E failures","description":"Playwright mobile projects fail because WizardLayout renders a hidden (md:block)

step title before page content. Tests use page.locator('h1').first(), which resolves to the hidden layout heading on mobile. Also results in multiple h1s per page. Fix by removing or demoting layout heading, ensuring a single visible h1 per wizard page, and update tests if needed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GreenDog","created_at":"2025-12-20T21:03:08.330927-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T21:30:35.67678-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T21:30:35.67678-05:00","close_reason":"Already fixed on main: apps/web/app/wizard/layout.tsx no longer renders a hidden

step title; only the step indicator remains, so mobile h1 selector mismatch is resolved.","labels":["a11y","ci","e2e","web"]} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dkhc","title":"Security: restore checksum verification for Atuin installer","description":"install.sh currently installs Atuin via a direct curl of GitHub releases/latest/download/atuin-installer.sh without checksums verification (regression vs acfs_run_verified_upstream_script_as_target). Update checksums.yaml + scripts/lib/security.sh to track the new URL (or revert to setup.atuin.sh) and ensure install.sh runs Atuin only via the verified-upstream wrapper; keep Atuin non-fatal.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:06:12.681145-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:06:21.160078-05:00"} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dkhc","title":"Security: restore checksum verification for Atuin installer","description":"install.sh currently installs Atuin via a direct curl of GitHub releases/latest/download/atuin-installer.sh without checksums verification (regression vs acfs_run_verified_upstream_script_as_target). Update checksums.yaml + scripts/lib/security.sh to track the new URL (or revert to setup.atuin.sh) and ensure install.sh runs Atuin only via the verified-upstream wrapper; keep Atuin non-fatal.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:06:12.681145-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:10:23.895407-05:00"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dmff","title":"Add headless browser auth flow explanation","description":"# Task: Add Headless Browser Auth Flow Explanation ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Authentication Flow for Headless VPS (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3edj) ## Description When users run `claude` or `gh auth login` on the VPS, these tools try to open a browser. But there's no browser on the VPS! The tool prints a URL that must be opened on the user's laptop. This "device flow" is completely unexplained. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/status-check/page.tsx Add explanation before the auth commands: \`\`\`tsx

Your VPS doesn't have a web browser, so authentication works a bit differently:

  1. Run the login command (like claude)
  2. The terminal will display a URL (or code)
  3. Copy that URL and paste it in your laptop's browser
  4. Complete the login in your browser
  5. Return to your terminal — it should confirm success
If you see "Opening browser..." but nothing happens, that's normal! Just copy the URL shown and open it manually.
\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Explains the device flow clearly - [ ] Step-by-step numbered list - [ ] Addresses "nothing happened" confusion - [ ] Appears before the auth command list - [ ] Works well on mobile ## UI/UX Notes - Use Laptop icon to reinforce "use your laptop's browser" - This should feel like a helpful heads-up, not a warning - Consider adding a visual diagram showing the flow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:42:00.199364-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:20.959969-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:20.959969-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: AlertCard with Laptop icon explaining device auth flow with 5-step process and 'Opening browser...' confusion addressed at lines 179-197"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dvt","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive unit coverage (no mocks) + E2E logging","description":"# Goal Establish real-data, no-mock unit coverage and complete E2E integration scripts with detailed, human-readable logging. # Why - Current tests are partial (installer E2E + web Playwright). No full unit coverage across core Bash libs or manifest tooling. - E2E logs exist but are not standardized or granular enough for fast diagnosis. # Success Criteria - Unit coverage exists for manifest parser/generator, selection resolver, contract/security/preflight libs with real fixtures (no mocks/fakes). - E2E scripts cover bootstrap + selection + resume flows with structured logs and artifacts. - Tests are deterministic, diffable, and runnable locally + in CI. # Notes - "No mocks/fakes" means: use real manifests, real generated scripts, real flags; avoid stubbing core behaviors. - Logging should be timestamped, sectioned, and include command echo + exit codes. ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:25:13.75871-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:16:59.6662-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:16:59.6662-05:00","close_reason":"All 6 children completed: coverage matrix, parser/generator tests (132 tests), installer lib tests (15 tests), test harness, selection E2E (26 tests), web E2E runner script.","labels":["qa","tests"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dvt.1","title":"Define coverage matrix + real fixtures catalog","description":"# Scope Create a concrete coverage matrix that maps each core component to a test type, and standardize a catalog of **real** fixtures used by unit tests. # Deliverables - Coverage matrix table (component → test type → owner → status). - Fixture catalog list (real manifest YAMLs, generated scripts, seed state files) with storage location and usage notes. # Detailed comments - Components to include: `packages/manifest` (schema/parser/generator), `scripts/lib` (install_helpers, contract, security, preflight, logging), `install.sh` selection/introspection, `tests/vm` E2E. - Fixtures must be real (valid YAMLs and generated outputs), not synthetic mocks. - Ensure fixtures are minimal but realistic; avoid brittle snapshots. # Acceptance criteria - Matrix is stored in repo (likely `docs/tests/coverage_matrix.md`). - Fixture catalog references actual files committed in repo. ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:25:21.951298-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:46:31.078442-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:46:31.078442-05:00","close_reason":"Added docs/tests/coverage_matrix.md and docs/tests/fixtures_catalog.md with real-file coverage matrix + fixture catalog","labels":["qa","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dvt.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dvt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:25:21.953283-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} @@ -265,9 +265,9 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nic8","title":"Task: Add 'skip for now' reassurance on accounts page","description":"","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:49:36.382899-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:49:36.382899-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nic8","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9dl9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:53:31.398245-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nna","title":"Add automatic retry for transient network errors","description":"# Task: Add automatic retry for transient network errors ## Purpose Handle temporary network issues gracefully without user intervention. ## Retry Strategy ### Timing: Immediate → 5s → 15s **Rationale for these intervals:** - **Immediate retry (0s):** Many transient errors clear instantly (TCP reset, brief DNS hiccup) - **5 second wait:** Enough for most CDN/routing issues to resolve - **15 second wait:** Handles rate limiting, brief outages - **Total wait:** 20 seconds max - fast enough to not frustrate users ```bash RETRY_DELAYS=(0 5 15) # Immediate, then 5s, then 15s retry_with_backoff() { local cmd="$1" local description="$2" local max_attempts=${#RETRY_DELAYS[@]} for ((attempt=0; attempt 0)); then log_info "Retry $attempt/$((max_attempts-1)) for $description (waiting ${delay}s)..." sleep "$delay" fi if eval "$cmd" 2>/dev/null; then ((attempt > 0)) && log_info "Succeeded on retry $attempt" return 0 fi done return 1 } ``` ### Retryable Errors Only retry for transient network issues: ```bash is_retryable_error() { local exit_code="$1" local stderr="$2" # curl exit codes for network issues case "$exit_code" in 6) return 0 ;; # Could not resolve host 7) return 0 ;; # Failed to connect 28) return 0 ;; # Operation timeout 35) return 0 ;; # SSL connect error 52) return 0 ;; # Empty reply 56) return 0 ;; # Network receive error esac # Check stderr for common transient messages if [[ "$stderr" =~ (timeout|timed.out|connection.refused|temporarily) ]]; then return 0 fi return 1 # Not retryable } ``` ### Integration with fetch_and_run() ```bash verified_fetch_and_run() { local url="$1" local checksum="$2" local name="$3" shift 3 retry_with_backoff \ "verify_checksum \"$url\" \"$checksum\" \"$name\"" \ "fetching $name" local status=$? if ((status \!= 0)); then log_error "Failed to fetch $name after ${#RETRY_DELAYS[@]} attempts" return 1 fi # Content already verified, now run verified_content | bash -s -- "$@" } ``` ## Non-Retryable Scenarios - HTTP 404 (script doesnt exist) - HTTP 403 (permission denied) - Checksum mismatch (not a network issue) - Script execution errors ## Dependencies - Used by: security.sh fetch functions - Used by: install.sh download operations ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Retry delays: 0s, 5s, 15s (total 20s max) - [ ] Only retries network-related curl errors - [ ] Clear logging of retry attempts - [ ] Does not retry checksum mismatches - [ ] Does not retry HTTP 4xx errors","notes":"Implementing retry-with-backoff for transient curl failures in scripts/lib/security.sh (0s/5s/15s).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:50:37.817313-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:02:52.336748-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:02:52.336748-05:00","close_reason":"Added retry-with-backoff (0/5/15s) for transient curl failures. Implemented acfs_curl_with_retry_and_sentinel() in scripts/lib/security.sh and wired into fetch_checksum()/verify_checksum(). Added the same retry logic to install.sh's acfs_fetch_url_content() and used it for remote checksums.yaml fetch.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nna","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qqo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:28.919648-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nq21","title":"Reconcile packages/onboard vs acfs/onboard divergence","description":"## Problem `packages/onboard/onboard.sh` and `acfs/onboard/onboard.sh` have diverged with different features: - `acfs/onboard/` (865 lines): Progress bar, celebration screen, timestamps - installed by `install.sh` - `packages/onboard/` (1049 lines): Auth services, different lesson titles array, `--cheatsheet` flag ## Analysis - **Canonical source**: `acfs/onboard/onboard.sh` (used by installer: `cp -r "$ACFS_DIR/onboard" "$ACFS_TARGET/"`) - **packages/onboard/**: Purpose unclear - may be stale or intended for different use ## Resolution Options 1. Delete `packages/onboard/` if unused 2. Make it a symlink to `acfs/onboard/` 3. Define clear build/sync process if both are needed ## Investigation Needed - Check if anything references `packages/onboard/` (package.json, scripts) - Understand original intent from AGENTS.md: 'Onboard TUI source' Raised by PinkLake in agent mail.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-22T15:44:11.583821-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:49:17.523019-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:49:17.523019-05:00","close_reason":"Reconciliation complete. packages/onboard/onboard.sh is canonical (1197 lines with all features). acfs/onboard/onboard.sh removed in commit c560234. Enhancements: - Interactive auth status checker component - Clear distinction between ChatGPT OAuth vs API keys - CAAM backup/restore workflow diagram - Warning callouts for common mistakes Key Content: ```markdown | Agent | Command | Alias | Company | |-------|---------|-------|---------| | Claude Code | `claude` | `cc` | Anthropic | | Codex CLI | `codex` | `cod` | OpenAI | | Gemini CLI | `gemini` | `gmi` | Google | ``` ### Lesson 5: NTM Command Center Introduces the core orchestration tool. Enhancements: - Visual session management diagram - `ntm spawn` command builder (optional interactive) - Session lifecycle visualization Key Content: - Session types and naming - Spawning agents - Attaching and detaching - Sending prompts ### Lesson 6: NTM Prompt Palette Teaches pre-built prompts and customization. Enhancements: - Searchable prompt gallery - Copy-to-clipboard for prompts - Category filtering (research, coding, review) ## Visual Components Needed 1. **AgentCard** - Shows agent status (authenticated/not) 2. **PromptCard** - Copyable prompt with syntax highlighting 3. **SessionDiagram** - Visual representation of ntm sessions ## Cross-Links - Link to wizard Step 7 (Set Up Accounts) for auth setup - Link to Reference Docs for command details - Link to Lesson 7 (Flywheel) for full workflow ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All 3 agent lessons accessible - [ ] Agent tables render correctly - [ ] Command examples have copy buttons - [ ] Warning callouts display prominently - [ ] Prompt examples are copyable - [ ] Mobile-friendly layout ## Dependencies Same as foundation lessons: - Lesson progress tracking (ori) - MDX rendering pipeline (25i) - Lesson layout and navigation (j9a)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:21:18.624349-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:26:23.584372-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:26:23.584372-05:00","close_reason":"All 9 lessons already load and render from source markdown files via lesson-content.tsx. Keep wording clear that older images are OK because installer upgrades.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:57:27.70072-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:59:43.497741-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:59:43.497741-05:00","close_reason":"Updated wizard OS guidance to 25.10 with auto-upgrade note"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-obi","title":"Implement Dicklesworthstone stack updates","description":"## What Implement the 'stack' category for acfs-update. This is the most complex category because these tools use verified installers. Tools to update: - NTM (Named Tmux Manager) - MCP Agent Mail - UBS (Ultimate Bug Scanner) - BV (Beads Viewer) - CASS (Coding Agent Session Search) - CM (CASS Memory System) - CAAM (Coding Agent Account Manager) - SLB (Simultaneous Launch Button) ## Technical Details - Most tools: Re-run installer from GitHub (handles update logic internally) - MCP Agent Mail: Special case - git pull + uv sync in ~/mcp_agent_mail - For updates (not fresh installs), we trust the GitHub repos directly - No checksum verification needed for updates - installer scripts are trusted ## Update Flow for Each Tool 1. Check if installed (binary exists) 2. Capture current version 3. Run installer with appropriate flags (e.g., --update, --yes) 4. Capture new version 5. Log the change ## Considerations - Unlike fresh installs, we don't verify checksums for updates - The installer scripts themselves handle idempotent updates - MCP Agent Mail is different (git-based, not binary releases) - Some tools may not have --update flag - reinstall is safe ## Success Criteria - [ ] All 8 stack tools update correctly - [ ] MCP Agent Mail git pull + uv sync works - [ ] Skips uninstalled tools - [ ] Version changes logged - [ ] No checksum errors (we bypass for updates)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:42.374382-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:32:19.419493-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T13:32:19.419493-05:00","close_reason":"Replaced by agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-d0g which includes proper checksum verification. The original 'bypass checksums' approach was a security vulnerability.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-obi","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:25.347073-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ofwh","title":"Wizard stepper: reflect markStepComplete updates without reload","description":"apps/web/lib/wizardSteps.ts has an imperative markStepComplete() that writes localStorage, but Stepper reads completion state via a React Query useQuery cache. Because the wizard layout stays mounted across routes, the query never refetches, so the Stepper can show stale completion state until reload. Fix by emitting an in-tab event when completed steps are saved and have useCompletedSteps listen + update query cache (and optionally listen to storage events for cross-tab sync).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:34:03.995739-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:40:12.835421-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T22:40:12.835421-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed: completed steps now dispatch event + useCompletedSteps listens to sync React Query cache (plus storage for cross-tab). Lint + type-check passed."} @@ -291,13 +291,13 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r50f","title":"State library: fail clearly on jq update errors","description":"scripts/lib/state.sh has several jq command substitutions that don’t check exit status (e.g., state_step_update/state_phase_complete/state_phase_fail/state_save/state_migrate_v1_to_v2/state_upgrade_set_error). In non-strict callers this can silently propagate bad state updates. Add explicit error handling so failures return nonzero without writing invalid/empty state.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:54:49.303573-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:59:19.847092-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T22:59:19.847092-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: added explicit jq failure handling in state.sh write/update helpers and initialized selection arrays early in install.sh; shellcheck + bash -n passed."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r56u","title":"TASK: Integrate acfs info into CLI and zshrc","description":"# TASK: Integrate acfs info into CLI and zshrc ## Context Wire up the info command into the existing acfs CLI function. ## Implementation ### Update acfs() function in acfs.zshrc ```bash acfs() { case "$1" in info) shift source "${ACFS_HOME:-$HOME/.acfs}/scripts/lib/info.sh" acfs_info "$@" ;; # ... existing cases esac } ``` ### Update acfs help output Add info to the help text: ``` acfs info Show environment quick reference acfs info --json Output as JSON acfs info --html Output as HTML ``` ### Create acfs_info dispatcher ```bash acfs_info() { case "$1" in --json) info_render_json ;; --html) info_render_html ;; --minimal) info_render_minimal ;; --help) info_show_help ;; *) info_render_terminal ;; esac } ``` ## Testing ```bash acfs info # Terminal output acfs info --json # JSON output acfs info --json | jq # Validate JSON acfs info --html > /tmp/test.html # HTML output acfs help # Shows info in list ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] acfs info works - [ ] acfs info --json works - [ ] acfs info --html works - [ ] acfs help shows info command - [ ] Help text is accurate","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:57:30.415862-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:07:45.631863-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:07:45.631863-05:00","close_reason":"Complete implementation: info.sh with terminal, JSON, HTML, minimal output formats; integrated into acfs CLI","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r56u","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-lwu7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:57:52.815793-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r6i3","title":"Fix acfs update: MCP Agent Mail tmux command argument splitting","description":"scripts/lib/update.sh uses tmux new-session with a single quoted string (" --dir ..."), so tmux tries to exec a binary with spaces. Pass command + args as separate argv entries (e.g., bash --dir /Users/jemanuel/mcp_agent_mail --yes).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:27:50.18235-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:31:32.626103-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T22:31:32.626103-05:00","close_reason":"Completed"} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r6xz","title":"Feature: SSH Key Guidance Enhancement","description":"","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:44:14.120191-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:06:16.488094-05:00"} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r6xz","title":"Feature: SSH Key Guidance Enhancement","description":"","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:44:14.120191-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:10:42.709063-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:10:42.709063-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented SSH Key Guidance Enhancement: added ~/.ssh folder explanation, enhanced file location guidance, and improved passphrase explanation in generate-ssh-key wizard page"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r7xo","title":"Default-collapse non-essential service tiers on accounts page","description":"# Task: Improve Accounts Page Information Hierarchy ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Authentication Flow for Headless VPS ## Problem The accounts page shows 8+ services at once, which is overwhelming. Even with tiers, seeing everything expanded makes users feel like they need ALL the services before proceeding. ## Current State - Essential tier: expanded (correct) - Recommended tier: expanded (should be collapsed) - Optional tier: expanded (should be collapsed) ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/accounts/page.tsx Change TIER_META defaults: \`\`\`tsx const TIER_META: Record = { essential: { // ... defaultOpen: true, // Keep open - these are required }, recommended: { // ... defaultOpen: false, // CHANGE: Collapse by default }, optional: { // ... defaultOpen: false, // Keep collapsed }, }; \`\`\` Also add clearer messaging: \`\`\`tsx Focus on Essential first. You only need GitHub and Claude Code to get started. The other services can wait until you need them — the tools will prompt you when it's time. \`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Recommended tier collapsed by default - [ ] Optional tier collapsed by default - [ ] Clear messaging that only Essential is needed now - [ ] Tiers still expandable for curious users - [ ] Progress bar only tracks Essential tier ## Why This Matters Overwhelming users with choices leads to decision paralysis and abandonment. A focused experience (2 services vs 8) is more achievable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:55:36.278776-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:02:53.516624-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:02:53.516624-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: accounts/page.tsx TierSection has defaultOpen:false for non-essential tiers"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rbrf","title":"Task: Add Linux skip option to OS selection","description":"","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:46:14.287345-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:46:14.287345-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rbrf","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qxj8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:43.784088-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rbu","title":"Implement auth status check functions for each service","description":"## Task Create functions to check authentication status for each service. ## Location `packages/onboard/src/lib/authChecks.ts` ## Implementation ```typescript import { execSync } from 'child_process'; import * as fs from 'fs'; import * as path from 'path'; import * as os from 'os'; export interface AuthStatus { authenticated: boolean; details?: string; // e.g., username, IP, etc. } /** * Check if Tailscale is authenticated and connected */ export function checkTailscale(): AuthStatus { try { const result = execSync('tailscale status --json', { encoding: 'utf-8' }); const status = JSON.parse(result); if (status.BackendState === 'Running') { const ip = execSync('tailscale ip -4', { encoding: 'utf-8' }).trim(); return { authenticated: true, details: `IP: ${ip}` }; } return { authenticated: false }; } catch { return { authenticated: false }; } } /** * Check if Claude Code is authenticated */ export function checkClaude(): AuthStatus { const configPaths = [ path.join(os.homedir(), '.claude', 'config.json'), path.join(os.homedir(), '.config', 'claude', 'config.json'), ]; for (const configPath of configPaths) { if (fs.existsSync(configPath)) { try { const config = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(configPath, 'utf-8')); if (config.user?.email) { return { authenticated: true, details: config.user.email }; } return { authenticated: true }; } catch { // File exists but unreadable, assume authenticated return { authenticated: true }; } } } return { authenticated: false }; } /** * Check if Codex CLI is authenticated */ export function checkCodex(): AuthStatus { const authPath = path.join(os.homedir(), '.codex', 'auth.json'); if (fs.existsSync(authPath)) { try { const auth = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(authPath, 'utf-8')); if (auth.access_token) { return { authenticated: true }; } } catch { // File corrupted } } return { authenticated: false }; } /** * Check if Gemini CLI is authenticated */ export function checkGemini(): AuthStatus { const credPaths = [ path.join(os.homedir(), '.config', 'gemini', 'credentials.json'), ]; // Also check for GOOGLE_API_KEY env var if (process.env.GOOGLE_API_KEY) { return { authenticated: true, details: 'via GOOGLE_API_KEY' }; } for (const credPath of credPaths) { if (fs.existsSync(credPath)) { return { authenticated: true }; } } return { authenticated: false }; } /** * Check if GitHub is configured */ export function checkGitHub(): AuthStatus { try { const result = execSync('gh auth status', { encoding: 'utf-8', stdio: 'pipe' }); if (result.includes('Logged in to')) { const match = result.match(/Logged in to .+ as (\S+)/); return { authenticated: true, details: match?.[1] }; } } catch { // Not authenticated } return { authenticated: false }; } /** * Check if Vercel CLI is authenticated */ export function checkVercel(): AuthStatus { try { const result = execSync('vercel whoami', { encoding: 'utf-8', stdio: 'pipe' }); if (result.trim()) { return { authenticated: true, details: result.trim() }; } } catch { // Not authenticated } return { authenticated: false }; } /** * Check if Supabase CLI is authenticated */ export function checkSupabase(): AuthStatus { const configPath = path.join(os.homedir(), '.supabase', 'config.toml'); if (fs.existsSync(configPath)) { return { authenticated: true }; } return { authenticated: false }; } /** * Check if Cloudflare/Wrangler is authenticated */ export function checkWrangler(): AuthStatus { try { const result = execSync('wrangler whoami', { encoding: 'utf-8', stdio: 'pipe' }); if (!result.includes('not authenticated')) { const match = result.match(/email: (.+)/i); return { authenticated: true, details: match?.[1] }; } } catch { // Not authenticated } return { authenticated: false }; } // Master check function export const AUTH_CHECKS: Record AuthStatus> = { tailscale: checkTailscale, 'claude-code': checkClaude, 'codex-cli': checkCodex, 'gemini-cli': checkGemini, github: checkGitHub, vercel: checkVercel, supabase: checkSupabase, cloudflare: checkWrangler, }; export function checkAllServices(): Record { const results: Record = {}; for (const [id, check] of Object.entries(AUTH_CHECKS)) { results[id] = check(); } return results; } ``` ## Testing Create unit tests: ```typescript // packages/onboard/src/lib/__tests__/authChecks.test.ts describe('authChecks', () => { it('checkTailscale returns correct status', () => { // Mock execSync // Test both authenticated and not authenticated cases }); // etc. }); ``` ## Considerations 1. **Error handling**: All checks should fail gracefully 2. **Performance**: Checks should be fast (no network calls) 3. **Cross-platform**: Handle macOS/Linux differences (onboard may run locally) 4. **Binary availability**: Don't error if binary not installed","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:02:18.880204-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:46:23.998833-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:46:23.998833-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented authChecks.ts and bun tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rbu","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8h4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:03:25.527838-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rbu","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-14w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:03:26.053877-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-re2","title":"Design AccountsStep UI component","description":"## Task Design and implement the AccountsStep wizard component. ## Location `apps/web/components/wizard/AccountsStep.tsx` ## Component Structure ```tsx // apps/web/components/wizard/AccountsStep.tsx import { useState, useEffect } from 'react'; import { SERVICES, Service, ServicePriority } from '@/lib/services'; import { ServiceCard } from './ServiceCard'; import { GoogleSsoCallout } from './GoogleSsoCallout'; import { useLocalStorage } from '@/hooks/useLocalStorage'; interface AccountsStepProps { onNext: () => void; onBack: () => void; } export function AccountsStep({ onNext, onBack }: AccountsStepProps) { const [checkedServices, setCheckedServices] = useLocalStorage( 'acfs-accounts-checked', [] ); const toggleService = (id: string) => { setCheckedServices(prev => prev.includes(id) ? prev.filter(s => s !== id) : [...prev, id] ); }; const priorityGroups: { priority: ServicePriority; title: string; description: string }[] = [ { priority: 'strongly-recommended', title: 'Strongly Recommended', description: 'Set these up first for the best experience', }, { priority: 'recommended', title: 'Recommended', description: 'High-value services for most users', }, { priority: 'optional', title: 'Optional', description: 'Nice to have, set up when needed', }, ]; const checkedCount = checkedServices.length; const totalCount = SERVICES.length; return (
{/* Header */}

Set Up Your Accounts

{/* Service Groups */} {priorityGroups.map(group => { const services = SERVICES.filter(s => s.priority === group.priority); if (services.length === 0) return null; return (

{group.title}

{group.description}

{services.map(service => ( toggleService(service.id)} /> ))}
); })} {/* Navigation */}
); } ``` ## Sub-Components ### ServiceCard ```tsx // apps/web/components/wizard/ServiceCard.tsx interface ServiceCardProps { service: Service; checked: boolean; onToggle: () => void; } export function ServiceCard({ service, checked, onToggle }: ServiceCardProps) { return (

{service.supportsGoogleSso && service.googleSsoUrl ? ( Sign up with Google ) : ( Sign up )} {service.alternativeAuth && ( )}
); } ``` ### GoogleSsoCallout ```tsx // apps/web/components/wizard/GoogleSsoCallout.tsx export function GoogleSsoCallout() { return (
💡

Pro Tip: Use Your Google Account

Most services support Google Sign-In. Using the same Google account for everything means fewer passwords and better security.

Make sure your Google account has{' '} 2-Step Verification enabled .

); } ``` ## Design Considerations 1. **Mobile-first**: Cards stack on mobile, 2-column on desktop 2. **Accessibility**: All interactive elements keyboard accessible 3. **Visual feedback**: Clear checked/unchecked states 4. **Non-blocking**: User can skip without creating all accounts 5. **Persistent state**: localStorage survives page reload 6. **External links**: Open in new tab with proper rel attributes ## Integration - Add to wizard step sequence in `apps/web/app/wizard/page.tsx` - Position after VPS setup, before "Run Installer" step","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:00:28.150149-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:30:23.960544-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:30:23.960544-05:00","close_reason":"Completed via accounts/page.tsx which includes: ServiceCard components, Google SSO callout, progress indicator, checkbox state, category grouping, skip/continue navigation. Page approach used instead of separate component file.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-re2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-14w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:03:24.723387-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rhvw","title":"TASK: Implement acfs info --json output","description":"# TASK: Implement acfs info --json output ## Context Add JSON output format for scripting and integration with other tools. ## Implementation ### Function ```bash info_render_json() { cat < "12+ services presented simultaneously... User thought: 'Do I really need ALL > of these? This is going to take hours!'" For beginners, seeing GitHub, Anthropic, OpenAI, Google AI, Cloudflare, Vercel, Supabase, and more creates: 1. **Decision Fatigue**: Which ones are truly required? 2. **Time Anxiety**: This looks like hours of work 3. **Abandonment Risk**: Users may give up or skip important ones ## Proposed Solution: Two-Tier Approach ### Tier 1: Essential (Show First, Required) Only 2-3 services that are TRULY needed to start: 1. **GitHub** - Required for code backup (the only backup!) 2. **Anthropic Claude** - Primary AI agent for the workflow That's it. Everything else can wait. ### Tier 2: Recommended (Collapsed, Do Later) Show these in a collapsed section: - **OpenAI** (for Codex CLI) - **Google AI** (for Gemini CLI) ### Tier 3: Optional (Even More Collapsed, When Needed) - Vercel (for deployment) - Cloudflare (for domains) - Supabase (for databases) - Docker Hub - etc. ## UI Design \`\`\` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Set Up Accounts │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ ⚡ ESSENTIAL (Do These Now) │ │ ───────────────────────────── │ │ These two accounts are required to start: │ │ │ │ [GitHub Card - expanded] │ │ [Anthropic Claude Card - expanded] │ │ │ │ ▶ RECOMMENDED (After First Project) ────────────── │ │ Add these when you want more AI agents │ │ │ │ ▶ OPTIONAL (When You Need Them) ───────────────── │ │ For deployment, databases, and more │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ \`\`\` ## Post-Install Authentication After each account is created, show the authentication command: ### For GitHub: 1. Sign up at github.com 2. On your VPS, run: \`gh auth login\` 3. Follow the prompts (browser flow) ### For Claude: 1. Subscribe to Claude Max at anthropic.com 2. On your VPS, run: \`claude\` 3. Complete browser login ## Verification UI Add verification step for each service: \`\`\` [✓] GitHub - authenticated [ ] Claude - needs authentication \`\`\` Could even check this programmatically: - GitHub: \`gh auth status\` returns 0 - Claude: check for ~/.claude/credentials or similar ## Data Model Updates Modify lib/services.ts: \`\`\`typescript interface Service { // ... existing fields tier: 'essential' | 'recommended' | 'optional'; sortOrder: number; // within tier } \`\`\` Then update SERVICES array with tier assignments. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Essential tier shows only GitHub and Claude - [ ] Recommended tier is collapsed by default - [ ] Optional tier is collapsed by default - [ ] Clear explanation of when to do each tier - [ ] Post-install auth commands shown for each service - [ ] Page loads faster (less to render initially) - [ ] Mobile experience improved (less scrolling) ## Dependencies - None - can be done in parallel with other features ## Migration Notes - lib/services.ts needs tier field added - Page component needs section logic - Existing service data preserved, just reorganized ## Success Metrics - Reduced time spent on accounts page - Higher completion rate for essential services - User feedback on cognitive load ## Considerations 1. **What about existing users?** They can still expand all sections 2. **What if user needs Vercel first?** They can expand Optional section 3. **International users?** Some may need different services first 4. **Enterprise users?** May need all services - that's fine, just expand","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:17:13.186334-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:39:53.635076-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:39:53.635076-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rn2q","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-umqa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:17:50.548497-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rs8j","title":"web: add commands+glossary links to Learning Hub","description":"Update `apps/web/app/learn/page.tsx` Quick Reference section to include links to the new `/learn/commands` and `/learn/glossary` pages (alongside existing Agent Commands / NTM). No new files.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:04:39.076357-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:05:53.643481-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:05:53.643481-05:00","close_reason":"Updated /learn Quick Reference cards to include /learn/commands and /learn/glossary."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-s25","title":"Create auth lesson flow in onboard TUI","description":"## Task Implement the interactive authentication lesson in the onboard TUI. ## Location `packages/onboard/src/lessons/auth/` ## Lesson Structure ### Main Entry (index.ts) ```typescript // packages/onboard/src/lessons/auth/index.ts import { Lesson, LessonStep } from '../../types'; import { checkAllServices, AUTH_CHECKS } from '../../lib/authChecks'; import { tailscaleLesson } from './tailscale'; import { claudeLesson } from './claude'; import { codexLesson } from './codex'; import { geminiLesson } from './gemini'; const serviceLessons = [ tailscaleLesson, claudeLesson, codexLesson, geminiLesson, ]; export const authLesson: Lesson = { id: 'auth', title: 'Authenticate Services', description: 'Connect your coding tools and services', async run(ui) { // Check current auth status const statuses = checkAllServices(); // Show overview ui.clear(); ui.header('Service Authentication'); ui.text('Let\'s connect your services. We\'ll go through each one.'); ui.blank(); for (const lesson of serviceLessons) { const status = statuses[lesson.serviceId]; const icon = status.authenticated ? '✓' : '○'; const detail = status.authenticated ? status.details || 'Authenticated' : 'Not configured'; ui.text(` ${icon} ${lesson.name.padEnd(15)} - ${detail}`); } ui.blank(); await ui.prompt('Press Enter to start...'); // Run each lesson for (const lesson of serviceLessons) { const status = statuses[lesson.serviceId]; if (status.authenticated) { // Already authenticated, ask if want to re-auth ui.clear(); ui.header(lesson.name); ui.success(`Already authenticated${status.details ? `: ${status.details}` : ''}!`); const choice = await ui.prompt('[Enter to continue, r to re-authenticate]'); if (choice.toLowerCase() !== 'r') { continue; } } await lesson.run(ui); // Re-check status after lesson const newStatus = AUTH_CHECKS[lesson.serviceId](); statuses[lesson.serviceId] = newStatus; } // Summary ui.clear(); ui.header('Authentication Complete!'); ui.blank(); const authenticated = Object.values(statuses).filter(s => s.authenticated).length; const total = Object.keys(statuses).length; ui.text(`${authenticated} of ${total} services authenticated.`); ui.blank(); for (const lesson of serviceLessons) { const status = statuses[lesson.serviceId]; const icon = status.authenticated ? '✓' : '✗'; ui.text(` ${icon} ${lesson.name}`); } ui.blank(); await ui.prompt('Press Enter to return to menu...'); }, }; ``` ### Individual Service Lessons ```typescript // packages/onboard/src/lessons/auth/tailscale.ts import { ServiceLesson } from '../../types'; import { checkTailscale } from '../../lib/authChecks'; export const tailscaleLesson: ServiceLesson = { serviceId: 'tailscale', name: 'Tailscale', async run(ui) { ui.clear(); ui.header('Tailscale Setup'); ui.blank(); ui.text('Tailscale gives you secure remote access to your VPS.'); ui.text('Once connected, you can SSH from anywhere!'); ui.blank(); ui.text('Run this command:'); ui.blank(); ui.code('sudo tailscale up'); ui.blank(); ui.text('This will open a browser (or show a URL).'); ui.text('Log in with your Google account.'); ui.blank(); const choice = await ui.prompt('[Press Enter when done, or s to skip]'); if (choice.toLowerCase() === 's') { ui.warn('Skipped. You can set up Tailscale later.'); await ui.sleep(1500); return; } // Verify authentication ui.text('Checking Tailscale status...'); await ui.sleep(1000); const status = checkTailscale(); if (status.authenticated) { ui.blank(); ui.success('Tailscale connected!'); ui.text(`Your Tailscale IP: ${status.details}`); ui.blank(); ui.text('Now you can SSH to this machine from any device'); ui.text('with Tailscale installed.'); ui.blank(); await ui.prompt('[Press Enter to continue]'); } else { ui.blank(); ui.warn('Tailscale not yet connected.'); ui.text('You can try again or set it up later.'); const retry = await ui.prompt('[r to retry, Enter to continue]'); if (retry.toLowerCase() === 'r') { return this.run(ui); // Recursive retry } } }, }; ``` ## UI Framework Use a simple TUI framework. Options: - `blessed` - Classic Node.js TUI - `ink` - React for CLIs - `prompts` - Simple and lightweight Recommend `prompts` + custom rendering for simplicity: ```typescript // packages/onboard/src/ui.ts import prompts from 'prompts'; import chalk from 'chalk'; export class UI { clear() { console.clear(); } header(text: string) { console.log(chalk.bold.blue(`\n${'═'.repeat(60)}`)); console.log(chalk.bold.blue(` ${text}`)); console.log(chalk.bold.blue(`${'═'.repeat(60)}\n`)); } text(text: string) { console.log(text); } blank() { console.log(); } code(text: string) { console.log(chalk.bgGray.white(` ${text} `)); } success(text: string) { console.log(chalk.green(`✓ ${text}`)); } warn(text: string) { console.log(chalk.yellow(`⚠ ${text}`)); } async prompt(message: string): Promise { const response = await prompts({ type: 'text', name: 'value', message, }); return response.value || ''; } async sleep(ms: number) { return new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, ms)); } } ``` ## Integration Add to main menu in `packages/onboard/src/index.ts`: ```typescript import { authLesson } from './lessons/auth'; const lessons = [ linuxBasicsLesson, terminalLesson, gitLesson, authLesson, // NEW agentLesson, ]; ```","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:02:59.962782-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:45:31.696507-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:45:31.696507-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in bash: onboard.sh has show_auth_flow() (lines 318-436) with full TUI menu, show_auth_service() for individual services, gum integration with fallback. Menu option [a] for 'Authenticate Services' added. Progress tracking and all 8 services supported.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-s25","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rbu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:03:25.780702-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-s25","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-bt5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:03:26.298833-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
